title: Write 'How to Add a New Concurrency Demo' contributor guide in CONTRIBUTING.md
description:
CONTRIBUTING.md has guides for adding nodes, algorithms, templates, and modules — but ZERO guidance for adding a new concurrency demo. A developer wanting to add 'Semaphore' must reverse-engineer the existing demos (3192-line file!) to figure out the 7-step process:

1. Create engine file: src/lib/concurrency/semaphore.ts (export simulateSemaphore)
2. Define step type: SemaphoreStep extending ConcurrencyStep
3. Export from: src/lib/concurrency/index.ts
4. Add demo ID to ConcurrencyDemo union type (ConcurrencyModule.tsx:62-73)
5. Add DemoDef entry to DEMOS array (ConcurrencyModule.tsx:81-148)
6. Add useMemo for simulation data in useConcurrencyModule hook
7. Add visualization component + switch case in canvas render
8. Add properties panel content for the new demo
9. Add bottom panel step log case
10. Add maxSteps case

Time wasted without guide: ~3-4 hours. Time with guide: ~30 minutes.

Add a 'How to Add a New Concurrency Demo' section to CONTRIBUTING.md with step-by-step instructions, file paths, and a checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: New section in CONTRIBUTING.md: 'How to Add a New Concurrency Demo'
Lists all 10 files/locations that need updating with exact paths
Includes a checklist: engine ✓, types ✓, index export ✓, DEMOS array ✓, useMemo ✓, viz ✓, properties ✓, bottomPanel ✓, maxSteps ✓, tests ✓
Includes a 'common mistakes' section (forgetting maxSteps case, not resetting stepIndex on demo switch)

title: Create concurrency demo scaffolding script: pnpm scaffold:concurrency-demo
description:
Adding a new concurrency demo requires creating/modifying 10+ locations across 4+ files. This is error-prone. Create a scaffolding script:

```bash
pnpm scaffold:concurrency-demo --name semaphore --title 'Semaphore'

Creates:
  src/lib/concurrency/semaphore.ts              (engine boilerplate with SemaphoreStep type)
  src/__tests__/lib/concurrency/semaphore.test.ts (test boilerplate)

Updates:
  src/lib/concurrency/index.ts                   (adds re-export)
  src/components/modules/ConcurrencyModule.tsx    (adds demo ID, DEMOS entry, switch cases)

Prints:
  ✓ Created semaphore engine + tests
  ✓ Registered in index.ts
  ✓ Added to ConcurrencyModule
  Next: implement simulateSemaphore() and create SemaphoreViz component
```

This saves 30+ minutes per new demo and eliminates 'forgot to add maxSteps case' bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Script creates engine file with correct boilerplate (type + simulate function stub)
Script creates test file with describe block and placeholder test
Script updates index.ts exports
Script prints clear 'next steps' for the developer
Script is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md

title: Add JSDoc documentation to all exported engine functions
description:
DX: 6 of 11 engine files have excellent file-level JSDoc (race-condition.ts, producer-consumer.ts, dining-philosophers.ts, goroutines.ts, event-loop.ts, mutex-comparison.ts). But 5 files have minimal or no function-level JSDoc: sleeping-barber.ts, readers-writers.ts, deadlock-demo.ts, async-patterns.ts, thread-lifecycle.ts.

Add @param, @returns, and @example JSDoc to all exported simulate functions. A developer hovering over `simulateReadersWriters` in their IDE should see:
```typescript
/**
 * Simulate a readers-writers scenario with configurable actor counts.
 * Uses readers-preference policy (writers may starve under heavy read load).
 *
 * @param readerCount - Number of reader actors (1-10)
 * @param writerCount - Number of writer actors (1-5)
 * @param rounds - Scheduling rounds per actor
 * @returns Ordered array of RWEvent for step-by-step playback
 *
 * @example
 * const events = simulateReadersWriters(3, 2, 4);
 * // events[0].action === 'read-start'
 */
```
acceptanceCriteria: All 15 exported simulate/compute functions have @param and @returns JSDoc
At least 5 functions include @example usage
JSDoc renders correctly in VS Code IntelliSense hover
Each function documents its synchronization policy or algorithm variant

title: Write architecture overview document for the Concurrency module
description:
No architecture document exists for the Concurrency module. A new developer opening the 3192-line ConcurrencyModule.tsx has no map. Create a concurrency-architecture.md (or JSDoc block) with:

1. Module architecture diagram (hook → sidebar + canvas + properties + bottomPanel)
2. Data flow: DEMOS → useMemo(simulate*) → stepIndex → Viz component
3. File organization: engines in lib/concurrency/, viz in ConcurrencyModule.tsx (currently), ThreadPoolSaturation in its own file
4. State management: 8 useState + 15 useMemo + 1 useRef in useConcurrencyModule
5. How to understand the module in 15 minutes (read order: DEMOS array → one engine → one viz → the hook)
6. Known architectural debt (monolithic file, no code splitting per viz)
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture document created (Markdown or JSDoc in useConcurrencyModule)
Includes data flow diagram with types at each step
Includes 'recommended reading order' for new developers
Lists architectural debt with links to relevant tasks

title: Add error boundary with developer-friendly error messages to concurrency visualizations
description:
If a visualization component crashes (e.g., undefined event at a step index), the entire module goes blank with no useful error. React error boundaries exist in the platform (ModuleRenderer uses them) but the error messages are generic.

Add a concurrency-specific error boundary wrapper:
```tsx
<ConcurrencyErrorBoundary demo={active} stepIndex={stepIndex}>
  {canvas}
</ConcurrencyErrorBoundary>
```

On error, show: 'ConcurrencyModule crashed while rendering [demo] at step [stepIndex]. Error: [message]. Try clicking Reset or switching to another demo.'

This saves developers 10+ minutes of debugging 'white screen' issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Error boundary wraps the canvas rendering area
Error message shows demo name, step index, and error details
Recovery actions visible: Reset button and demo switcher
Error is logged to console with full stack trace

title: Create PR review checklist specific to concurrency demo contributions
description:
When a developer submits a PR adding a new concurrency demo, reviewers need a checklist to verify quality. Create a .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/concurrency-demo.md:

## Concurrency Demo PR Checklist
- [ ] Engine file exports simulateXxx() function
- [ ] Step type extends ConcurrencyStep base (if created)
- [ ] Exported from lib/concurrency/index.ts
- [ ] Demo ID added to ConcurrencyDemo union type
- [ ] DemoDef entry added to DEMOS array with clear description
- [ ] useMemo computation added with appropriate deps
- [ ] Visualization component renders all step states
- [ ] Properties panel has: hook, intuition, technical explanation
- [ ] Bottom panel step log shows current step description
- [ ] maxSteps case returns correct array length
- [ ] Step descriptions explain WHY, not just WHAT
- [ ] At least 3 unit tests written
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors (use design tokens)
- [ ] Reduced motion respected (no infinite animations)
- [ ] All text readable (minimum fontSize 10 in SVG, text-[10px] in Tailwind)
acceptanceCriteria: PR template created with 15+ checklist items
Template auto-applied when PR title contains 'concurrency' or '[CON]'
Checklist items are specific and verifiable (not vague)

title: Add Storybook stories for concurrency visualization components
description:
Storybook exists (.storybook/main.ts + preview.ts) but no concurrency stories are written. Add stories for at least the 4 A/B-grade visualizations so developers can iterate on visual changes without running the full app:

1. GoroutineViz.stories.tsx — render each of 5 demos at step 0, mid-step, and final step
2. DeadlockDemoViz.stories.tsx — render normal state + deadlock state
3. EventLoopViz.stories.tsx — render each demo at step 0 and final step
4. LockComparisonViz.stories.tsx — render all 3 lock types at step 0 and mid-step

Each story should use mock data (not full simulation) so it renders instantly.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 story files created for visualization components
Each story renders without errors in Storybook
Stories use mock step data (not full simulation engine)
Stories show multiple states: initial, mid-simulation, and final/deadlock
